tool_input.summary: To assess whether Arabia Terra deposits reflect a single fluvial style or multiple depositional systems, you’ll want (1) regional-to-local orbital images to map channel/fan/delta geometries and stratigraphic relationships, and (2) topography/DTMs to quantify slopes, basin morphology, and longitudinal profiles. The PDS catalog results below are the most directly useful Mars imaging/topography archives I found for that purpose (no Arabia-Terra-specific dedicated dataset was returned—these are global/regional products you can subset by location).

candidates.title: VOLUME MROX_0001: MRO CTX EDR ARCHIVE
candidates.reasoning: CTX (~6 m/pixel) image coverage is a workhorse for mapping fluvial landforms across Arabia Terra (valley networks, inverted channels, fan/delta morphologies, channel patterns). Use for regional geomorphic mapping and to identify candidate sites representing different depositional styles.

candidates.title: MEX-M-HRSC-3-RDR-V4.0
candidates.reasoning: HRSC radiometrically processed image products support regional mapping of depositional architectures in Arabia Terra at intermediate resolution; useful for connecting local CTX-scale observations to basin-scale context.

candidates.title: MEX-M-HRSC-5-REFDR-MAPPROJECTED-V3.0
candidates.reasoning: Map-projected HRSC products simplify GIS-based mapping of channel/fan/delta planforms and stratigraphic contacts across Arabia Terra, helping test for diversity vs. uniformity of fluvial styles over wide areas.

candidates.title: MEX-M-HRSC-5-REFDR-DTM-V1.0
candidates.reasoning: HRSC-derived DTMs enable slope, relief, and longitudinal-profile analyses needed to distinguish depositional system types (e.g., alluvial fans vs. deltas vs. throughgoing fluvial systems) and to evaluate whether deposits fit one process regime or multiple.

candidates.title: MGS-M-MOLA-5-MEGDR-L3-V1.0
candidates.reasoning: MOLA gridded topography provides the basin-scale elevation framework for Arabia Terra: drainage divides, gradient fields, base levels, and regional accommodation space—key to interpreting whether observed deposits require multiple depositional settings.

candidates.title: MGS-M-MOLA-5-IEGDR-L3-V2.0
candidates.reasoning: MOLA interpolated gridded products can be useful as an alternative/companion topographic surface for regional geomorphic analyses (e.g., smoothing choices) when testing system-scale hypotheses across Arabia Terra.

candidates.title: MOLA Topography Data in IMG Format
candidates.reasoning: PDS4-packaged MOLA topography products (served with MARSIS/geomorph context) are convenient for workflows that prefer PDS4 collections; provides elevation context supporting basin-scale interpretation of depositional system diversity.

candidates.title: MOLA Topography Images in IMG and PNG Format
candidates.reasoning: Quicklook/topography image products help rapid reconnaissance of regional settings within Arabia Terra (basins, intercrater plains, scarps) before doing detailed mapping that can reveal multiple fluvial/depositional styles.

You are a dataset-discovery assistant for the NASA PDS catalog (atm, geo, img, naif, ppi, rms, sbn nodes, ~12k datasets).

Tools are provided via MCP. You MUST call at least one search tool before returning results.

Interpret the user's science question, infer the relevant mission(s) and instrument(s), then search using mission/instrument keywords — not raw science terms alone. Return both PDS4 and PDS3 versions when available. Only return candidates you've seen in tool results — never invent IDs. Stay under ~10 tool calls. If nothing matches, return an empty list.
Do the Arabia Terra deposits record only one fluvial style or a diverse range of depositional systems?
